title: phanera kingii
intro: Phanera kingii, also known as the golden rain tree, is a species of climbing vine in the family Euphorbiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as lowering blood pressure, reducing fever, and treating respiratory issues, making it a valuable resource in folk medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of extracts, decoctions, or infusions, and its leaves, bark, and roots are commonly used in traditional remedies.
